sources.notes: Official tutorial uses divide-and-conquer/randomized grouping to isolate the hidden element with high probability.

annotation.summary: Find a hidden special element using interactive queries under the easier query/probability limits.
annotation.constraints: The query budget permits recursive splitting or repeated randomized tests.
annotation.core_idea: Maintain a candidate set. Query a balanced subset or randomized bucket; the response tells whether the hidden singleton is inside that subset with enough reliability. Recurse on the indicated half/bucket, repeating when necessary to amplify success probability.
annotation.complexity: O(log n) to O(log^2 n) interactive queries depending on amplification.

solution_variants.name: randomized-divide-and-conquer
solution_variants.summary: Repeatedly split or bucket candidates and use query outcomes to keep the bucket containing the hidden singleton.

tags.evidence: The task is an interactive hidden-element search.
The candidate set is recursively split based on query responses.
The strategy relies on randomized buckets and success-probability amplification.
